title: Questions That Need Multiple Retrievals
concept: Queries where the answer to one sub-question is required before the next retrieval can even be formed.

exercises.prompt: What is the primary purpose of Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) in LLM applications?

options.text: Retrieving relevant documents from a database to ground the LLM's response.
Fine-tuning the LLM's internal weights on new domain data.
Translating natural language queries directly into raw SQL code.

exercises.explain: To understand multi-hop retrieval, we must first look at standard RAG, which retrieves documents from a vector database to help the LLM answer a query with external context.

cards.title: The Hidden Clue Problem
cards.body: Standard search engines fail when one question is hidden inside another. To answer complex questions, AIs need to perform a chain-reaction of searches, using the first answer to unlock the next query.

cards.title: What is Multi-Hop Retrieval?
cards.body: Multi-Hop RAG is a technique where an AI breaks down a complex question, retrieves information for the first part, and uses that new information to search for the next part. Without this, standard single-shot searches fail because they don't know what to look for next.

cards.title: The Scavenger Hunt Analogy
cards.body: Think of it like a scavenger hunt where each clue is sealed. You cannot search for Clue B until you find and open Clue A to read the directions.

cards.title: Step-by-Step in Action
cards.body: If you ask 'Who is the spouse of the director of Inception?', a standard search looks for 'spouse director Inception' all at once. A multi-hop system first retrieves the director (Christopher Nolan) and then performs a second search for 'spouse of Christopher Nolan'.

cards.title: Myth: Bigger Prompts Solve Everything
cards.body: Many believe that simply feed-forwarding a complex prompt to a database will return the right answer. In reality, if the key search terms rely on intermediate answers, a single-step search will miss the target entirely.

cards.title: Direct Jump vs. Stepwise Climbing
cards.body: Standard RAG tries to jump directly to the answer in one leap, whereas Multi-Hop RAG takes calculated, sequential steps.

left.text: Searches for 'spouse of director of Inception' in one go, often returning irrelevant results about the movie cast.

right.text: Finds 'Christopher Nolan' first, then searches for his spouse, ensuring an accurate final answer.

exercises.prompt: Why does traditional single-hop RAG fail on questions like "What is the capital of the country where the writer of 'Norwegian Wood' was born?"

options.text: It uses database indexing to speed up the response.
It only does one search step, failing when the second search term is unknown.
It requires millions of parameters to run.

exercises.explain: Standard RAG retrieves documents in a single step using the raw query, which fails when the query contains hidden dependencies that must be resolved first.

exercises.prompt: True or False: In a multi-hop RAG system, all search queries can be executed simultaneously in parallel to save time.
exercises.explain: Multi-hop queries must be resolved sequentially because the terms for the second query are not known until the first query returns a result.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to describe the relationship between search steps in multi-hop RAG.
exercises.sentence: A multi-hop query creates a search dependency because you cannot run the second query without the first answer.
exercises.explain: In multi-hop retrieval, the output of the first search acts as a prerequisite for the second, creating a strict dependency between steps.

exercises.prompt: Identify which agent output represents an automated multi-hop query formulation plan.

options.text: AI Search Planner: 'Step 1: Retrieve writer of Norwegian Wood. Step 2: Use retrieved name [Haruki Murakami] to search for birth country. Step 3: Search for capital of that country.'
User: 'Can you tell me who wrote Norwegian Wood and where they were born?'
Database: 'Haruki Murakami, born in Kyoto, Japan, wrote Norwegian Wood in 1987.'

exercises.explain: Option B mimics a human-written question, and Option C is a simple look-up. Option A is characteristic of an AI orchestrating a multi-hop retrieval plan by generating intermediate search queries.

exercises.prompt: Imagine you are building a customer support assistant. Which of the following queries represents a true multi-hop retrieval task where the second search depends on the result of the first?

options.text: Find the CEO of the company that won the 2023 Clean Energy Award.
List the top three clean energy companies of 2023.
Summarize the biography of the CEO of Tesla.
Compare the 2023 revenues of Tesla and Ford.

exercises.explain: A multi-hop query requires solving an intermediate query first because the parameters of the second search depend entirely on the output of the first. Searching for the CEO of a company whose name you don't yet know is the classic definition of a multi-hop dependency.
